prompt:
The lot-sheet module for our auction floor stopped compiling after I untangled this morning's merge, and the compiler is gaslighting me. First error:

  LotSheet.java:74: error: reached end of file while parsing
  }
   ^

Line 74 is the last line of the file. It's a closing brace. It has been a closing brace since March. After that javac goes on to claim `cannot find symbol: method add(Lot)` about thirty times, but add() is RIGHT THERE in the class, I'm looking at it. I diffed the merge and every hunk looks reasonable to me.

Nothing about lot ordering was supposed to change in this merge — ring order, reserve order, tie-breaks, all of it should behave exactly as it always has, and TestMain.java pins that behavior. Get `java TestMain.java` compiling and green with the smallest change you can find. Don't restructure the class and don't touch TestMain.java.
